black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

description: The lac operon is a classic example of prokaryotic gene regulation discovered by Jacob and Monod (Nobel Prize 1965). This detailed flowchart demonstrates the computational logic of bacterial gene regulation, showing how environmental inputs (lactose, glucose, energy status) are processed through regulatory logic gates (both OR and AND) to control gene expression and metabolic pathways. The system illustrates transcriptional control through the lac repressor, CAP-cAMP complex, and RNA polymerase, ultimately regulating lactose metabolism.
changelog_v2: V2 aligned with RegulonDB: LacI represses lacZ, lacY, lacA; CRP activates lacZ, lacY, lacA, and lacI. Added explicit node 'LacI gene expression' with edge from CAP Binds Promoter (Z) to reflect CRP (+) activation of lacI.

name: Lac Operon Regulation
notes: V2 RegulonDB-backed: regulator–gene edges aligned with RegulonDB NetworkRegulatorGene (LacI − lacZ/Y/A; CRP + lacZ/Y/A, lacI). This flowchart represents maximum complexity with 64 unique nodes (A-III + ANDGATE1 + ANDGATE2), each with distinct identifiers. Contains 5 OR logic gates (orange) and 2 AND logic gates (lavender). The explicit AND gates properly represent multi-signal integration: (1) High cAMP requires low energy AND no glucose, (2) Strong transcription requires operator free AND CAP bound. Final system states (Energy Production, Lactose Consumption, Cell Survival, Homeostasis, System Equilibrium) are colored blue as cellular states/conditions. Transcriptional outcomes (EE, FF, GG) are colored violet as functional outputs. The model includes environmental sensing, transport mechanisms, regulatory logic gates, transcription control, protein synthesis, metabolic functions, and feedback regulation. All molecular components and regulatory mechanisms have been validated against primary research sources.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified biochemical and genetic data from peer-reviewed literature and aligned with RegulonDB (v13) for regulator–gene edges. All molecular components, regulatory mechanisms, and metabolic pathways have been validated against primary research sources and the RegulonDB backbone. The model represents the current scientific consensus on lac operon regulation. Logic gates accurately represent the Boolean logic of transcriptional regulation.
